abstract: This study investigates the integration of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) within higher education, focusing specifically on the institutional landscape of the University of Guayaquil. The research aimed to identify the primary GAI tools utilized by faculty while assessing the core benefits and obstacles associated with their adoption. Employing a mixed-methods approach, the study combined an extensive literature review with surveys administered to 334 faculty members across diverse academic departments. The analysis indicates that ChatGPT remains the most prevalent tool, though its adoption rate varies considerably across different disciplines. While participants identified significant advantages—most notably the ability to create personalized learning materials and streamline lesson planning—the results also highlight critical barriers, such as institutional resistance to change and a widespread lack of formal training. This study concludes that while generative AI holds transformative potential for the academic environment, its successful implementation depends on strategic institutional planning, continuous professional development, and the establishment of clear ethical guidelines to govern its pedagogical use.
